ddls:
CREATE TABLE "drivers" (
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each driver */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the driver */,
  "bitness" TEXT /* Bitness of the driver (e.g., 32-bit, 64-bit) */,
  "released" TEXT /* Release date of the driver */,
  "file_size" REAL /* File size of the driver in MB */,
  "download_link" TEXT /* URL for downloading the driver */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the driver */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Supported operating system(s) */,
  "hardware_requirements" TEXT /* Hardware requirements for the driver */,
  "software_requirements" TEXT /* Software requirements for the driver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the driver this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the driver */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of this specific version */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding changes or updates in this version */,
  "change_log" TEXT /* Detailed change log for this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the driver this file is associated with */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the file in MB */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., executable, zip, text) */,
  "checksum" TEXT /* Checksum for file integrity verification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_files_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, IT support) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the driver */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the driver was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., download, view) */,
  "access_mode" TEXT /* Mode of access (e.g., direct, API) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver being reviewed */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback provided */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given to the driver (1-5 scale) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the issue is resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for permission entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this permission applies to */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated driver */,
  "can_download" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user can download the driver */,
  "can_view" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user can view the driver details */,
  "can_manage" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user can manage the driver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "download_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each download request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the request */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver requested for download */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date of the download request */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the request (e.g., completed, pending) */,
  "request_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the request originated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_download_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_download_requests_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "driver_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for category entry */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver this category belongs to */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Graphics, Virtualization) */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_driver_categories_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vendor_details" (
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vendor */,
  "vendor_name" TEXT /* Name of the vendor providing the driver */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the vendor */,
  "website" TEXT /* Vendor's website URL */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vendor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "driver_vendor_relation" (
  "relation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the relation entry */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver */,
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vendor */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., developer, distributor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_driver_vendor_relation_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_driver_vendor_relation_vendor_id FOREIGN KEY ("vendor_id") REFERENCES vendor_details ("vendor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed (e.g., download, update) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action was performed */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the driver this metric belongs to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., downloads, views) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_keys" (
  "key_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API key entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user this API key belongs to */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* The actual API key */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the API key expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("key_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_keys_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
